content: For as long as I can remember, a girl named Lila appeared every night in my dreams, always smiling like an old friend. We shared secrets and adventures there, and I never imagined she was anything but a figment of my imagination. Years later, as an adult, I saw her again—this time in a crowded café—and she looked right at me, eyes wide with recognition. I called out, but she quickly looked away and slipped out the door without a word. Determined to understand, I followed her for days, but every time I tried to approach, she vanished like a ghost. Finally, in a quiet moment, she whispered, “You’re not supposed to find me,” before disappearing again. I started digging into local legends and found stories of dreamwalkers—people who live between dreams and reality. Could Lila be one of them, trapped between worlds, or simply a part of my mind reaching out for help? I don’t have the answers yet, but meeting her again has changed how I see dreams—and reality—forever. Now, I keep watching the shadows, wondering if some friendships are meant to cross the boundary between sleep and waking life.
